environment.display_strategy: Xvfb with Openbox, xterm, xdotool, wmctrl, scrot; isolated HOME/XDG_CONFIG_HOME/XDG_DATA_HOME/XDG_STATE_HOME/XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/WARP_DATA_PROFILE/WARP_LOCAL_CONTROL_DISCOVERY_DIR per enabled/disabled profile.
environment.permission_enablement: Settings > Scripting equivalent private preferences were pre-seeded in isolated user_preferences.json because the validation profile is non-user and onboarding was precompleted. This produced real Warp local-control discovery records and app-side enforcement.

caveats: file.list returned an empty list because no CodeView/editor tabs were open in the terminal-only validation state. The current CLI exposes `file list` only, and `file open` is not accepted, so a non-empty file context could not be created through warpctrl setup.

A first active-window block.list attempt failed with missing_target while xterm owned focus. Subsequent validation used a delayed invocation plus explicit Warp refocus/click, satisfying active-window target resolution while retaining staggered screenshots.

commands.title: enabled block list
commands.permission_state: outside-Warp control enabled; metadata reads enabled; underlying-data reads enabled; app-state/config/underlying-data mutations disabled
commands.proof_setup_question: What is the best way to show the impact of this CLI command?
commands.proof_setup_answer: Warp was launched in an isolated Xvfb/Openbox session with onboarding precompleted. Recognizable commands created terminal blocks/history, and input text WARPCTRL_INPUT_SENTINEL_f61caf49 was left in the input box. The setup screenshot shows the sentinel block output, pwd output, history sentinel, and input sentinel.
commands.expected_result: JSON list of recent blocks including the sentinel command block.
commands.actual_result: Returned five block summaries including printf WARPCTRL_BLOCK_SENTINEL_f61caf49; exit 0.

visual_inspection.expected_visual_effect: The Warp window should show sentinel terminal content proving there are blocks/history and input text to read, while the external terminal shows full/representative JSON output for the command.
visual_inspection.observed_visual_effect: Screenshots show the external xterm with command/output and the Warp terminal with WARPCTRL_BLOCK_SENTINEL_f61caf49, pwd output, WARPCTRL_HISTORY_SENTINEL_f61caf49, and WARPCTRL_INPUT_SENTINEL_f61caf49 visible.
visual_inspection.unexpected_ui_changes: None. Warp remains in the same terminal workspace; active focus highlighting changes as expected when refocused.

commands.title: enabled file list
commands.permission_state: outside-Warp control enabled; metadata reads enabled; underlying-data reads enabled; app-state/config/underlying-data mutations disabled
commands.proof_setup_question: What is the best way to show the impact of this CLI command?
commands.proof_setup_answer: Warp was launched in an isolated Xvfb/Openbox session with onboarding precompleted. Recognizable commands created terminal blocks/history, and input text WARPCTRL_INPUT_SENTINEL_f61caf49 was left in the input box. The setup screenshot shows the sentinel block output, pwd output, history sentinel, and input sentinel.
commands.expected_result: JSON open-file metadata for any files visible/open in Warp.
commands.actual_result: Returned {"files": []}; exit 0. No file editor tab was open in the terminal-only validation state.

visual_inspection.expected_visual_effect: External terminal should show file list JSON, and Warp should show the current terminal-only state that explains why no editor files are visible.
visual_inspection.observed_visual_effect: Screenshot shows terminal-only Warp state and JSON files: [] in xterm. No open code editor/file tab is visible.
visual_inspection.ambiguity: The command passed, but visual evidence cannot prove a non-empty file list because this CLI slice exposes no file open subcommand and the terminal-only state has no editor file tabs.

commands.notes: Exploratory `warpctrl file --help` showed only `file list`; `warpctrl file open --help` returned unrecognized subcommand, so a meaningful non-empty file context could not be created using warpctrl in this slice.

commands.title: disabled block list
commands.permission_state: outside-Warp control enabled; metadata reads enabled; underlying-data reads disabled; app-state/config/underlying-data mutations disabled
commands.proof_setup_question: What is the best way to show the impact of this CLI command?
commands.proof_setup_answer: Separate isolated Warp profile with underlying-data reads disabled. Visible terminal state contains WARPCTRL_DISABLED_SENTINEL_f61caf49 block output and WARPCTRL_DISABLED_INPUT_SENTINEL_f61caf49 in the input box.
commands.expected_result: Because block.list is cataloged as metadata read, it should still succeed with metadata reads enabled and underlying-data reads disabled.
commands.actual_result: Returned block summaries including WARPCTRL_DISABLED_SENTINEL_f61caf49; exit 0.

visual_inspection.expected_visual_effect: The Warp window should show a disabled-profile sentinel terminal/input state while the external terminal shows denial or metadata-read output.
visual_inspection.observed_visual_effect: Screenshots show the external xterm and the disabled-profile Warp terminal with WARPCTRL_DISABLED_SENTINEL_f61caf49 and WARPCTRL_DISABLED_INPUT_SENTINEL_f61caf49 visible.

commands.notes: This verifies permission isolation: disabling underlying-data reads does not block metadata-read block summaries.

commands.title: initial block list focus attempt
commands.permission_state: outside-Warp control enabled; metadata reads enabled; underlying-data reads enabled; app-state/config/underlying-data mutations disabled
commands.proof_setup_question: What is the best way to show the impact of this CLI command?
commands.proof_setup_answer: Warp was launched in an isolated Xvfb/Openbox session with onboarding precompleted. Recognizable commands created terminal blocks/history, and input text WARPCTRL_INPUT_SENTINEL_f61caf49 was left in the input box. The setup screenshot shows the sentinel block output, pwd output, history sentinel, and input sentinel.
commands.expected_result: Attempt active-window read with Warp visible.
commands.actual_result: Returned missing_target because Warp app active-window state had not updated while xterm owned focus; exit 1.

visual_inspection.expected_visual_effect: The screenshot should show the focus conflict: xterm visible with missing_target while Warp target state is visible behind it.
visual_inspection.observed_visual_effect: Screenshot shows xterm output missing_target and Warp terminal visible behind it; subsequent delayed/refocused cases resolved the issue.

commands.notes: Exploratory focus-model attempt; superseded by delayed/refocus strategy in case 011.
